description: Overwriting or reverting a user's unrelated in-progress edit while making a scoped change, because the working tree was not inspected first.
body:
Failure mode: the agent edits a file (or runs a formatter/codemod) and in doing so discards uncommitted user changes that were unrelated to the task, because it treated the file as if the committed version were ground truth.

Detection signals: git status showed modified files before editing that the change did not account for; a diff touches lines the task never mentioned; a user edit present at session start is absent afterward.

Do not use: do not edit a file without first reading its current working-tree state; do not run repo-wide formatters or codemods over files with uncommitted user work; do not assume the committed version is what the user is looking at.

Safer fallback: collect git ground truth first (git status + diff), scope the edit to only the lines the task requires, and if unrelated user changes are present, preserve them and surface them rather than overwriting.
